title: glaucium corniculatum subsp. phoeniceum
intro: Glaucium corniculatum subsp. phoeniceum, also known as red horn poppy or red horn gromwell, is a perennial plant species belonging to the poppy family Papaveraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as glaucine and protopine. The health benefits of this plant include relief from pain, swelling, and inflammation, making it a potential treatment for conditions like arthritis and gout. The herbal preparations of Glaucium corniculatum subsp. phoeniceum typically involve the use of its roots and flowers, which can be dried and processed into teas, tinctures, or capsules for medicinal purposes.
